content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — `NSCameraUsageDescription` is generic ("This app needs camera access.") and `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription` is a placeholder ("TODO"); Info.plist lines for both usage keys.
Pierre: Apple rejects apps whose purpose strings are empty, generic, or leftover placeholders because users can't understand why access is requested. The camera string doesn't mention document scanning (the actual feature in ScannerView.swift), and the location string is literally "TODO" — and no code even requests location, so the key may not belong at all. Rewrite the camera string to reference scanning documents, and either add a real location string tied to a visible feature or remove `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription` entirely.
